title: Agent Check Bootstrap
competency: 01. Toolchain Setup - Project rules, hooks, guardrails, and CLI or MCP wiring
domain: Agent-safe local toolchain gate before CI evidence exists
mission: Turn a fragile manual setup into a repeatable agent-safe local gate with rules, hooks, allowed tools, and clear verification commands.
outcome: The starter no longer depends on manual verification; CI can produce a reviewable evidence bundle.
seededDefects: agent can run build without typecheck
rules file names a stale test command
no hook blocks access to local secrets
agentWorkflow: Ask the coding agent to inspect this lab contract, starter code, docs, and tests before proposing a plan.
Revise the agent plan so it exercises the competency practice and avoids the common mistake.
Implement the smallest working change that addresses the seeded defects.
Run the verification gates and capture command evidence before writing the final review note.
workingDeliverables: CI workflow or `scripts/ci-check` that runs locally.
Evidence artifact generator with real command output.
At least one app or test change needed to make the gate meaningful.
A PR evidence summary generated by the script.
masterySignals: Add a local `agent:check` toolchain command that runs install validation, lint/format/typecheck/build, and guardrail simulation.
Add or update AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md rules so the agent knows safe commands, forbidden paths, and the verification gate.
Implement deny rules or a PreToolUse-style hook simulator for secrets, destructive git commands, and unapproved CLIs.
Codify one repeated correction as a durable rule and prove the agent gate catches it.
